notes:
DOWNSCOPED by rq4.5 revision: API is always proxied and media is never CORS-gated, so direct-vs-proxy probing is moot for API. Remaining value: auto-fallback of MEDIA loads to the proxy stream path on media error (exotic servers), scheme rejection already in connect UI. Reassess before building.
2026-07-06 follow-up: transport made per-connection via connect-time probe (direct-first, proxy fallback) — tailnet (*.ts.net) and localhost servers now supported via direct browser calls (require ALLOW_CORS=1 on ABS; env var flaky in some ABS versions, hence probing). Verified: mock-CORS direct e2e + demo-server proxy e2e, suite 33/33.
close_reason: Downscoped per rq4.5 revision (API always proxied; media not CORS-gated): implemented the remaining value — one-shot media-error direct->proxy reload preserving position; scheme rejection already in connect UI. Deep probe/persisted-transport dropped as moot.

title: E1.7 Multi-track playback engine (global position map, gapless src swap)
description: audioTracks[] from POST /api/items/{id}/play each carry contentUrl/startOffset/duration/mimeType. Build track map: global position <-> (trackIndex, offsetInTrack). Single primary <audio>; swap src at track end; preload next track (hidden second element or warm fetch) for near-gapless. Seek resolves through the map (may swap track + set currentTime). Scrubber/chapters/ghosts operate in global position space. Mobile only handles audioFiles[0] — streaming must not repeat that gap.
acceptance_criteria: A 3-file MP3 book plays across boundaries with correct scrubber domain and cross-boundary seek.
close_reason: multi-track map + dual-audio role swap w/ preload; e2e: 66-file book, 25-min seek lands correct track ±15s, boundary auto-advance keeps playing
